rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bell Sound Usa Llc Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2021, line 1. Defendant Bell Sound Usa Llc Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action for interference with contractual relations, including alleging facts (not conclusions) of Defendant's knowledge of the existence and the terms of the contract; facts addressing statute of frauds; and facts addressing fair competition privilege. The Court notes that neither the statute of frauds not the fair competition issues raised in the demurrer were addressed by Plaintiff in opposition. All other causes of action/remedies asserted against this defendant are derivative of the interference with contractual relations. They should only be re-alleged if defendant can in good faith allege a cause of action for interference with contractual relations. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cbre, Inc., Erroneously Sued As Cbre Capital Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2021, line 1. Defendant Cbre, Inc., Erroneously Sued As Cbre Capital Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is SUSTIANED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege all elements of breach of contract with this defendant, i.e. contract formation and breach of specific terms (whether independent contract or "multi-party" contract Plaintiff to allege each element as to this defendant). See Warington Lumber Co. v. Fullerton Mortg. & Escrow Co. (1963) 222 Cal. App. 2d 706. As to the promissory estoppel cause of action, Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to this Defendant. Plaintiff shall further allege fraud with particularity as to this Defendant. For declaratory relief cause of action, to the extent it may be based on claims to property, Plaintiff to allege this Defendant's interest in the property. As to all other causes of action and/or remedies, they depend on viability of contract-based and fraud causes of action and shall be re-alleged only if those causes of action are properly asserted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cim Group, L. P., 246 First Street, (Sf) Owner, Llc Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2021, line 1. Defendant Cim Group, L. P., 246 First Street, (Sf) Owner, Llc Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of each contract-based and promissory estoppel causes of action. Plaintiff shall also allege facts addressing the Statute of Frauds. Plaintiff shall allege fraud with requisite particularity. As to all other causes of action and/or remedies, they depend on viability of contract-based and fraud causes of action and shall be re-alleged only if those causes of action are properly asserted. The Court will not consider Ng Declaration and Exhibit(s) thereto for the purpose of this demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cim Group, L. P., 246 First Street, (Sf) Owner, Llc Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2021, line 1. Defendant Cim Group, L. P., 246 First Street, (Sf) Owner, Llc Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint is moot. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL SLADE VS. JEREMY GARDNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 6, 2021, line 2. DEFENDANT KINNARD HOCKENHULL DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Complaint alleges that the tenant violated the lease and all occupancy in possession under the rental agreement is terminated. Complaint further alleges that this tenancy is subject to SFRO, i.e. not subject to TPA 2019. See CC 1946.2 (g)(2). = (501/REQ)
